pattern: The greatest common divisor of an array reduces to the gcd of just its minimum and maximum values, because any divisor of every element must already divide both of those extremes.
hints: You don't need every element to compute this -- only two of them matter.
The smallest and the largest values in the array are the only two numbers whose greatest common divisor you need to find.
Find the minimum and maximum of the array, then compute the greatest common divisor of just those two numbers.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, gcd is neither extreme
extreme values at the bounds of the range
middle elements share a large common factor with each other but not with the extremes
the maximum is an exact multiple of the minimum

stress.description: random arrays across the full value range
